PROJECT 49: THE FULL OBSERVABILITY STACK — END-TO-END PIPELINE MONITORING

🏗️ HYPOTHETICAL PROBLEM

Your company operates a Kafka-based event mesh processing 2.3 billion
events daily across 47 microservices, 400+ topics, and 3 Kafka clusters.

THE OBSERVABILITY NIGHTMARE THAT TRIGGERED THIS PROJECT:

Monday 9:14 AM:
→ Customer reports: "My order placed 45 minutes ago still shows pending"

Monday 9:16 AM:
→ Support escalates to engineering
→ Engineer checks order-processor consumer lag: 0 (looks fine)
→ Engineer checks payment-processor lag: 0 (looks fine)
→ Engineer checks notification-sender lag: 0 (looks fine)

Monday 9:22 AM:
→ Engineer finds the order in the database: status = "payment_pending"
→ Checks payment service logs: no record of this order
→ Question: "Where did the payment event go?"

Monday 9:35 AM:
→ After 20 minutes of log searching across 6 services:
→ Discovery: The order event was produced to orders-events topic
→ It was consumed by order-processor (confirmed via logs)
→ order-processor produced a payment-request to payments-events
→ payment-processor consumed it... but the enrichment step
   called the user-profile service which was returning 503
→ Circuit breaker opened → message went to spillover topic
→ Spillover consumer was paused (Project 48 backpressure)
→ Message sitting in payments-events-spillover for 45 minutes

Monday 9:38 AM:
→ Root cause found. Total investigation time: 24 minutes
→ Customer waited 45 minutes + 24 minutes = 1 hour 9 minutes
→ For a problem that should have been detected in 30 seconds

LEADERSHIP DEMAND:
→ Trace any message from producer to final consumer in < 30 seconds
→ Automatic alerting when end-to-end latency exceeds SLA
→ Correlation: given an order ID, show every Kafka event it touched
→ Predict consumer lag problems BEFORE they impact customers
→ Data lineage: for any topic, show upstream sources and downstream sinks
→ SLA dashboard: real-time view of pipeline health commitments
→ Zero manual log searching — everything in one place

🔍 HOW TO APPROACH IT

DECOMPOSE THE OBSERVABILITY PROBLEM:

Observability has THREE pillars (industry standard):

Pillar 1 — METRICS (covered partially in Projects 28, 41)
├── Numeric measurements over time
├── Consumer lag, throughput, latency percentiles
├── Broker health, partition distribution
├── Already have Prometheus + Grafana from Project 28
└── NEED: End-to-end latency metrics, SLA tracking

Pillar 2 — LOGS (partially covered throughout)
├── Structured event logs from each service
├── Already logging in each project
├── NEED: Centralized log aggregation
├── NEED: Correlation IDs linking logs across services
└── NEED: Structured JSON logging with trace context

Pillar 3 — TRACES (NEW — this project's primary focus)
├── Distributed traces showing message flow across services
├── Producer → Broker → Consumer → Downstream
├── Each hop is a "span" in the trace
├── Spans linked by trace ID propagated via Kafka headers
├── OpenTelemetry is the industry standard
└── NEED: Full implementation from scratch

ADDITIONAL OBSERVABILITY LAYERS:

Layer 4 — CORRELATION
├── Given a business ID (order_id), find all related events
├── Across topics, consumer groups, services
├── Requires consistent correlation ID propagation
└── NEED: Correlation ID middleware for all producers/consumers

Layer 5 — PREDICTION
├── Don't just report lag — predict when it will become critical
├── Trend analysis on consumer lag
├── Anomaly detection on throughput patterns
├── NEED: Lag prediction algorithm

Layer 6 — LINEAGE
├── For any topic: what produces to it? What consumes from it?
├── Static analysis (code/config) + dynamic analysis (runtime)
├── Data flow graph across entire platform
└── NEED: Topic lineage registry

Layer 7 — SLA TRACKING
├── Business-level commitments: "orders processed within 5 minutes"
├── Maps technical metrics to business outcomes
├── Tracks compliance percentage over time
└── NEED: SLA definition and tracking system

WHAT CONNECTS TO PREVIOUS PROJECTS:

→ Project 28: JMX metrics, Prometheus, Grafana (foundation)
→ Project 41: Interceptors for injecting tracing (hooks)
→ Project 44: Complex event processing that needs tracing
→ Project 48: Backpressure events that need visibility

🛠️ HOW CAN WE SOLVE IT

ARCHITECTURE: UNIFIED OBSERVABILITY PLATFORM

┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     KAFKA EVENT MESH                              │
│                                                                   │
│  ┌──────────┐     ┌──────────┐     ┌──────────┐                 │
│  │ Producer  │────▶│  Topic   │────▶│ Consumer │                 │
│  │ Service A │     │          │     │ Service B│                 │
│  │           │     │          │     │          │                 │
│  │ ┌───────┐ │     │          │     │ ┌───────┐│     ┌────────┐ │
│  │ │Tracer │ │     │          │     │ │Tracer ││────▶│ Topic  │ │
│  │ │Plugin │ │     │          │     │ │Plugin ││     │        │ │
│  │ └───┬───┘ │     │          │     │ └───┬───┘│     │        │ │
│  └─────┼─────┘     └──────────┘     └─────┼────┘     └────────┘ │
│        │                                   │                      │
│        │    trace_id + span_id             │                      │
│        │    propagated via                 │                      │
│        │    Kafka headers                  │                      │
│        │                                   │                      │
└────────┼───────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────┘
         │                                   │
         ▼                                   ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              OPENTELEMETRY COLLECTOR                               │
│  ┌────────────────┐  ┌───────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────┐ │
│  │ Trace Receiver  │  │ Metric        │  │ Log Receiver        │ │
│  │ (OTLP)         │  │ Receiver      │  │ (OTLP)              │ │
│  └───────┬────────┘  └──────┬────────┘  └──────────┬──────────┘ │
│          │                   │                       │            │
│  ┌───────▼───────────────────▼───────────────────────▼──────────┐│
│  │                    PROCESSING PIPELINE                        ││
│  │  ┌──────────┐  ┌────────────┐  ┌──────────────────────┐     ││
│  │  │ Batch    │  │ Correlation│  │ SLA Calculation      │     ││
│  │  │ Processor│  │ Enricher   │  │ Processor            │     ││
│  │  └──────────┘  └────────────┘  └──────────────────────┘     ││
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘│
└───────┬───────────────────┬───────────────────┬──────────────────┘
        │                   │                   │
        ▼                   ▼                   ▼
┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────────────┐
│   Jaeger /   │  │  Prometheus  │  │  Elasticsearch /     │
│   Tempo      │  │              │  │  Loki                │
│  (Traces)    │  │  (Metrics)   │  │  (Logs)              │
└──────┬───────┘  └──────┬───────┘  └──────────┬───────────┘
       │                 │                      │
       └─────────────────┼──────────────────────┘
                         │
                         ▼
              ┌──────────────────┐
              │     GRAFANA      │
              │                  │
              │ ┌──────────────┐ │
              │ │ Trace View   │ │
              │ │ Metric Dash  │ │
              │ │ Log Explorer │ │
              │ │ SLA Dashboard│ │
              │ │ Lineage Map  │ │
              │ └──────────────┘ │
              └──────────────────┘


MESSAGE FLOW WITH TRACING:

1. Producer creates message:
   → Generate trace_id (if none exists)
   → Create producer span
   → Inject trace context into Kafka headers:
     - traceparent: 00-{trace_id}-{span_id}-01
     - correlation_id: ORDER-12345
     - source_service: order-api

2. Kafka broker stores message:
   → Headers travel with the message
   → No broker modification needed

3. Consumer receives message:
   → Extract trace context from Kafka headers
   → Create consumer span (child of producer span)
   → Process message
   → If producing downstream: propagate same trace_id

4. OpenTelemetry Collector receives spans:
   → Assembles complete trace from all spans
   → Calculates end-to-end latency
   → Checks SLA compliance
   → Exports to Jaeger/Tempo for visualization

✅ WHAT COULD BE DONE

OPTION A: CUSTOM TRACING (Build from Scratch)
├── Implement own trace ID propagation
├── Custom header format
├── Custom trace storage and visualization
├── Maximum control but massive engineering effort
└── Verdict: Reinventing the wheel

OPTION B: OPENTELEMETRY (Industry Standard — CHOSEN)
├── W3C Trace Context standard (traceparent header)
├── Rich SDK for Python, Java, Go, etc.
├── Kafka-specific instrumentation available
├── Exports to any backend (Jaeger, Tempo, Zipkin)
├── Active open-source community
└── Verdict: Best for production systems

OPTION C: VENDOR-SPECIFIC (Datadog, New Relic, etc.)
├── Turn-key solution
├── Kafka integration available
├── Expensive at scale (2.3B events/day)
├── Vendor lock-in
└── Verdict: Good if budget allows, bad for learning

WE CHOOSE: OPTION B — OpenTelemetry
→ Standard-based, vendor-neutral
→ Works with our existing Prometheus + Grafana (Project 28)
→ Can add vendor backend later without code changes
→ Interceptor pattern (Project 41) integrates naturally

✅ WHAT SHOULD DO

1. PROPAGATE W3C TRACE CONTEXT VIA KAFKA HEADERS
   → Standard header: "traceparent"
   → Format: 00-{trace_id}-{span_id}-{flags}
   → Every producer injects, every consumer extracts
   → Same trace_id flows through entire pipeline

2. ADD CORRELATION ID TO EVERY MESSAGE
   → Business-level ID: order_id, user_id, transaction_id
   → Separate from trace_id (trace changes, correlation doesn't)
   → Header: "correlation_id"
   → Enables "find all events for this order" queries

3. USE INTERCEPTORS FOR TRANSPARENT INSTRUMENTATION
   → Project 41 interceptors inject tracing automatically
   → Application code doesn't change
   → onSend() creates producer span
   → onConsume() creates consumer span

4. CALCULATE END-TO-END LATENCY AS METRIC
   → Produce timestamp → final consumer timestamp
   → Export as Prometheus histogram
   → P50, P95, P99 latency per pipeline

5. IMPLEMENT LAG PREDICTION
   → Track lag trend (increasing/decreasing/stable)
   → Linear regression on recent lag data points
   → Predict "time to breach SLA" based on trend
   → Alert BEFORE breach, not after

6. BUILD TOPIC LINEAGE REGISTRY
   → Register producer → topic → consumer relationships
   → Update dynamically from interceptor data
   → Visualize as directed graph

7. DEFINE SLAs AS CODE
   → YAML definitions: "orders processed within 5 minutes"
   → Automated compliance tracking
   → Real-time SLA dashboard

8. USE STRUCTURED JSON LOGGING WITH TRACE CONTEXT
   → Every log line includes trace_id, span_id, correlation_id
   → Enables jumping from trace → logs → metrics
   → Standard format across all services

❌ WHAT SHOULD NOT DO

1. DO NOT TRACE EVERY SINGLE MESSAGE IN HIGH-THROUGHPUT TOPICS
   → 2.3B events/day × trace overhead = massive storage cost
   → Use sampling: trace 1% of messages in normal topics
   → Trace 100% of critical paths (payments, errors)
   → Adaptive sampling: increase during incidents

2. DO NOT PUT LARGE PAYLOADS IN TRACE SPANS
   → Span attributes should be metadata, not full message body
   → Include: topic, partition, offset, key, message_size
   → Exclude: full message value (too large, possibly sensitive)

3. DO NOT MAKE TRACING SYNCHRONOUS AND BLOCKING
   → Trace export must be async (batch export)
   → Trace failure must NOT fail message processing
   → If tracing is down, messages still flow

4. DO NOT USE TRACE STORAGE AS EVENT STORE
   → Traces have retention (7-30 days typically)
   → Not a replacement for Kafka's own retention
   → Use for debugging, not for replay

5. DO NOT IGNORE CLOCK SKEW BETWEEN SERVICES
   → Different machines have different clocks
   → Spans may appear out of order
   → Use NTP synchronization across all nodes
   → OpenTelemetry handles clock skew in trace assembly

6. DO NOT COUPLE SLA TRACKING TO SINGLE METRIC
   → "Consumer lag < 10K" is not an SLA
   → SLA is business outcome: "order confirmed within 5 minutes"
   → May require multiple metrics combined

7. DO NOT FORGET TO TRACE SPILLOVER AND RETRY PATHS
   → Messages in spillover topics (Project 48) still need tracing
   → Retry topic messages need same trace context
   → Otherwise: message "disappears" from trace view

8. DO NOT ALERT ON EVERY LAG SPIKE
   → Transient spikes during rebalance are normal
   → Alert on sustained lag exceeding prediction threshold
   → Use prediction, not just current value

🔀 WHEN TO USE WHAT

┌────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ QUESTION                       │ TOOL                                 │
├────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┤
│ "Where did this message go?"   │ Distributed tracing (trace_id)      │
│                                │ → Jaeger/Tempo trace view            │
├────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┤
│ "Show all events for order X"  │ Correlation ID search                │
│                                │ → Elasticsearch/Loki query           │
├────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┤
│ "Is the pipeline healthy?"     │ Metrics dashboard                    │
│                                │ → Grafana with Prometheus             │
├────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┤
│ "Will we breach SLA soon?"     │ Lag prediction + SLA tracker         │
│                                │ → Predictive alerting                 │
├────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┤
│ "What feeds into this topic?"  │ Topic lineage registry               │
│                                │ → Lineage graph visualization        │
├────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┤
│ "Why is this consumer slow?"   │ Trace spans + metrics correlation    │
│                                │ → Span duration breakdown            │
├────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┤
│ "What happened at 3 AM?"       │ Structured logs with trace context   │
│                                │ → Loki log exploration               │
├────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┤
│ "Are backpressure events       │ Backpressure metrics (Project 48)    │
│  affecting SLAs?"              │ + SLA correlation dashboard          │
├────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┤
│ "How does the DR failover      │ Cross-cluster trace propagation      │
│  affect latency?" (Proj 47)    │ + E2E latency histogram             │
└────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────┘

🚫 WHEN NOT TO USE

1. DEVELOPMENT/TEST ENVIRONMENTS (FULL STACK)
   → Full observability stack is expensive to run
   → Use simplified tracing (console exporter)
   → Metrics to stdout, not Prometheus
   → Deploy full stack only in staging + production

2. BATCH PROCESSING PIPELINES
   → Tracing is designed for request-response and streaming
   → Batch jobs have different observability patterns
   → Use job-level metrics instead of per-message tracing

3. WHEN TRACING OVERHEAD EXCEEDS 1% OF THROUGHPUT
   → At 2.3B events/day, even small overhead adds up
   → If tracing causes measurable latency: reduce sampling
   → Tracing should be invisible to message processing

4. SENSITIVE DATA ENVIRONMENTS WITHOUT MASKING
   → Trace spans may capture message keys/headers
   → PII in headers → PII in trace storage
   → Implement attribute masking before enabling tracing

5. SIMPLE SINGLE-SERVICE PIPELINES
   → If producer and consumer are in the same service
   → Internal metrics are sufficient
   → Distributed tracing adds value at 3+ services

⚙️ HOW IT ONLY WORKS (INTERNAL MECHANICS)

OPENTELEMETRY TRACE PROPAGATION VIA KAFKA HEADERS:

W3C Trace Context Standard (traceparent header):

Format: {version}-{trace_id}-{parent_span_id}-{trace_flags}
Example: 00-4bf92f3577b34da6a3ce929d0e0e4736-00f067aa0ba902b7-01

  version:        "00" (always)
  trace_id:       32-char hex (128-bit) — unique per end-to-end flow
  parent_span_id: 16-char hex (64-bit) — unique per operation
  trace_flags:    "01" = sampled, "00" = not sampled


TRACE FLOW THROUGH KAFKA:

Service A (Producer)                    Kafka                     Service B (Consumer)
─────────────────────                   ─────                     ──────────────────────

1. Create root span:
   trace_id: abc123
   span_id:  span_A1
   name: "orders-events send"

2. Inject into headers:
   msg.headers = [
     ("traceparent",                                              
      "00-abc123-span_A1-01"),
     ("correlation_id",
      "ORDER-5678"),
     ("source_service",
      "order-api"),
   ]

3. producer.send(msg) ─────────▶  4. Stored with headers  ──────▶  5. consumer.poll()
                                                                     
                                                                  6. Extract from headers:
                                                                     traceparent → abc123, span_A1
                                                                     
                                                                  7. Create child span:
                                                                     trace_id: abc123 (SAME)
                                                                     span_id:  span_B1 (NEW)
                                                                     parent:   span_A1
                                                                     name: "orders-events receive"
                                                                     
                                                                  8. Process message
                                                                     Create processing span:
                                                                     trace_id: abc123 (SAME)
                                                                     span_id:  span_B2 (NEW)
                                                                     parent:   span_B1
                                                                     
                                                                  9. Produce downstream:
                                                                     Inject trace context again
                                                                     trace_id: abc123 (SAME)
                                                                     span_id:  span_B3 (NEW)


RESULTING TRACE TREE:

trace_id: abc123
├── span_A1: "orders-events send" (Service A, 2ms)
│   └── span_B1: "orders-events receive" (Service B, 145ms)
│       ├── span_B2: "process_order" (Service B, 120ms)
│       │   ├── span_B2a: "db_insert" (Service B, 15ms)
│       │   └── span_B2b: "validate_payment" (Service B, 95ms)
│       └── span_B3: "payments-events send" (Service B, 3ms)
│           └── span_C1: "payments-events receive" (Service C, 200ms)
│               ├── span_C2: "charge_card" (Service C, 180ms)
│               └── span_C3: "notifications-events send" (Service C, 2ms)


END-TO-END LATENCY CALCULATION:

E2E latency = timestamp(last_consumer_span.end) - timestamp(first_producer_span.start)

For ORDER-5678:
  Producer span start: 2024-01-15T09:14:00.000Z
  Final consumer end:  2024-01-15T09:14:00.350Z
  E2E latency:         350ms

This is exported as a Prometheus metric:
  kafka_pipeline_e2e_latency_seconds{
    pipeline="order-to-notification",
    correlation_id="ORDER-5678"
  } = 0.350


LAG PREDICTION ALGORITHM:

Simple linear regression on lag time series:

Given lag samples: [(t1, lag1), (t2, lag2), ..., (tn, lagn)]

1. Calculate slope (rate of lag change):
   slope = Σ((ti - t̄)(lagi - laḡ)) / Σ((ti - t̄)²)

2. If slope > 0: lag is increasing
   Predict time to breach:
   time_to_breach = (breach_threshold - current_lag) / slope

3. If slope ≤ 0: lag is stable or decreasing
   No breach predicted

Example:
  Current lag: 50,000 records
  Slope: +2,000 records/minute
  SLA threshold: 100,000 records
  Time to breach: (100,000 - 50,000) / 2,000 = 25 minutes

Alert rule:
  IF time_to_breach < 10 minutes → WARN
  IF time_to_breach < 5 minutes  → CRITICAL


SAMPLING STRATEGY:

Not all messages need tracing. Sampling reduces overhead:

1. HEAD-BASED SAMPLING (decision at producer):
   → Random: trace 1% of messages
   → Rule-based: trace 100% of payments, 1% of analytics
   → Consistent: same order_id always sampled or not

2. TAIL-BASED SAMPLING (decision at collector):
   → Collect all spans initially
   → After trace completes: decide to keep or drop
   → Keep: traces with errors, high latency, specific attributes
   → Drop: normal traces
   → Requires buffering at collector (more expensive)

WE USE: Head-based with rules
  → payments.* topics: 100% sampling
  → orders.* topics: 10% sampling
  → analytics.* topics: 1% sampling
  → Any message with error: 100% sampling (force sample)

🚨 COMMON PITFALLS AND FAILURE MODES

PITFALL 1: TRACE CONTEXT LOST AT SERVICE BOUNDARY
├── Problem: Consumer reads trace from headers but doesn't propagate
│            when producing to next topic
├── Impact: Trace breaks — appears as two separate traces
├── Symptom: "I can see the producer span but not the consumer"
├── Solution: Always extract + inject at every produce/consume boundary
└── Use interceptors (Project 41) to make this automatic

PITFALL 2: TRACE STORAGE FILLS UP
├── Problem: High-throughput topics generate millions of traces
├── Impact: Jaeger/Tempo runs out of disk, queries become slow
├── Symptom: "Traces older than 2 hours are missing"
├── Solution: Sampling + retention policies
└── Tip: Tempo with object storage (S3) scales better than Jaeger

PITFALL 3: CORRELATION ID NOT PROPAGATED CONSISTENTLY
├── Problem: Some services set correlation_id, others don't
├── Impact: "Find all events for ORDER-5678" returns partial results
├── Symptom: Missing events in correlation query
├── Solution: Mandatory correlation_id in producer interceptor
└── Reject messages without correlation_id at gateway

PITFALL 4: CLOCK SKEW MAKES TRACES LOOK WRONG
├── Problem: Service A's clock is 2 seconds ahead of Service B
├── Impact: Consumer span appears to START before producer span
├── Symptom: "This trace timeline doesn't make sense"
├── Solution: NTP synchronization on all nodes
└── OpenTelemetry collectors can adjust for known skew

PITFALL 5: TRACING OVERHEAD IN HOT PATH
├── Problem: Span creation and export adds latency to message processing
├── Impact: 5% throughput reduction on high-volume topics
├── Symptom: Consumer lag increases after enabling tracing
├── Solution: Async span export, batch processing, sampling
└── Measure: Compare throughput with/without tracing enabled

PITFALL 6: LAG PREDICTION FALSE ALARMS
├── Problem: Temporary spike causes prediction to fire
├── Impact: Alert fatigue — team ignores predictions
├── Symptom: "We get 50 lag prediction alerts a day"
├── Solution: Require sustained trend (not just 2 data points)
└── Use 5-minute rolling average, not instantaneous values

PITFALL 7: SLA METRIC DOESN'T MATCH BUSINESS REALITY
├── Problem: SLA says "99.9% within 5 minutes" but measures wrong thing
├── Impact: Dashboard shows green but customers complain
├── Symptom: "SLA says 99.95% but NPS score is dropping"
├── Solution: Validate SLA metric with actual customer experience
└── Include end-to-end latency from API request to confirmation

PITFALL 8: LINEAGE GRAPH BECOMES STALE
├── Problem: Services change topics but lineage registry not updated
├── Impact: Lineage shows wrong dependencies
├── Symptom: "Lineage says service A produces to topic X but it doesn't"
├── Solution: Dynamic lineage from interceptor data, not static config
└── Prune entries not seen in 7 days

📈 SCALABILITY CONSIDERATIONS

TRACE STORAGE SCALING:

VOLUME CALCULATION:
→ 2.3B events/day
→ 1% sampling for most topics = 23M traces/day
→ Average trace: 5 spans × 500 bytes = 2.5KB per trace
→ Daily storage: 23M × 2.5KB = ~57.5GB/day
→ 30-day retention: ~1.7TB
→ With 100% sampling on payments (10% of traffic):
  230M × 2.5KB = ~575GB/day → need selective sampling

COLLECTOR SCALING:
→ 1 OTel collector handles ~10,000 spans/second
→ At 1% sampling: 23M traces × 5 spans / 86400s ≈ 1,330 spans/s
→ 1-2 collector replicas sufficient for 1% sampling
→ For 100% sampling on critical topics: scale collectors

PROMETHEUS SCALING:
→ Custom metrics add cardinality
→ E2E latency per pipeline: ~20 time series
→ Lag prediction per consumer group: ~50 time series
→ SLA metrics per SLA definition: ~30 time series
→ Total additional: ~100 time series (negligible for Prometheus)

LOG AGGREGATION SCALING:
→ Structured JSON logs: ~1KB per log line
→ 47 services × 1000 logs/s = 47,000 logs/s
→ Daily: ~4TB of raw logs
→ Use Loki (log aggregation) with S3 backend
→ Retain 14 days = ~56TB

SAMPLING STRATEGY AT SCALE:
┌──────────────────────┬──────────┬───────────────────────┐
│ Topic Category        │ Rate     │ Sampling              │
├──────────────────────┼──────────┼───────────────────────┤
│ payments.*           │ 50K/s    │ 100% (all critical)   │
│ orders.*             │ 200K/s   │ 10%                   │
│ inventory.*          │ 100K/s   │ 5%                    │
│ notifications.*      │ 150K/s   │ 1%                    │
│ analytics.*          │ 500K/s   │ 0.1%                  │
│ Error messages       │ varies   │ 100% (always trace)   │
│ High-latency (>SLA)  │ varies   │ 100% (always trace)   │
└──────────────────────┴──────────┴───────────────────────┘

🔍 INTERPRETABILITY CONSIDERATIONS

OBSERVABILITY DASHBOARD HIERARCHY:

LEVEL 1: EXECUTIVE SLA DASHBOARD
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  🏢 KAFKA PLATFORM — SLA COMPLIANCE                         │
│                                                               │
│  ┌─────────────────┐ ┌─────────────────┐ ┌────────────────┐ │
│  │ Order Pipeline   │ │ Payment Pipeline│ │ Notification   │ │
│  │ SLA: 5 min      │ │ SLA: 30 sec    │ │ SLA: 2 min     │ │
│  │ Current: 99.97% │ │ Current: 99.91%│ │ Current: 99.85%│ │
│  │ ● GREEN         │ │ ● GREEN        │ │ ● YELLOW       │ │
│  └─────────────────┘ └─────────────────┘ └────────────────┘ │
│                                                               │
│  30-Day SLA Trend:                                           │
│  ▁▁▂▁▁▁▁▁▃▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▂▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▅▁▁  (each bar = 1 day)     │
│                                                               │
│  Predicted Breaches: 0 in next 60 minutes                    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

LEVEL 2: ENGINEERING PIPELINE DASHBOARD
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  ⚙️ ORDER PIPELINE — DETAILED VIEW                           │
│                                                               │
│  End-to-End Latency (P50/P95/P99):                          │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐                    │
│  │ P50: 120ms  P95: 450ms  P99: 1.2s  │                    │
│  │ ▄▃▄▅▄▃▄▃▅▄▃▆▄▃▄▅▇▄▃▄▃▄▃▄▃▅▄▃▄   │                    │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘                    │
│                                                               │
│  Pipeline Stages:                                            │
│  API → orders-events → order-processor → payments-events    │
│       │    12ms      │     95ms        │      8ms           │
│       → payment-processor → notifications-events            │
│         │     180ms       │      5ms                        │
│         → notification-sender → DONE                        │
│           │     45ms          │                              │
│                                                               │
│  Consumer Lag Prediction:                                    │
│  order-processor:    Lag=1.2K  Trend: ↓ stable              │
│  payment-processor:  Lag=5.8K  Trend: ↑ +1K/min             │
│                      ⚠️ Predicted breach in 47 min            │
│  notification-sender: Lag=890  Trend: ↓ stable              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

LEVEL 3: TRACE INVESTIGATION VIEW
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  🔍 TRACE: abc123def456 | Correlation: ORDER-5678           │
│                                                               │
│  Timeline:                                                   │
│  ├─ order-api: send orders-events (2ms)                     │
│  │  ├─ order-processor: receive orders-events (145ms)       │
│  │  │  ├─ validate_order (5ms)                              │
│  │  │  ├─ enrich_order (25ms)                               │
│  │  │  │  └─ user-profile-api call (20ms)                   │
│  │  │  ├─ db_insert (15ms)                                  │
│  │  │  └─ send payments-events (3ms)                        │
│  │  │     ├─ payment-processor: receive (200ms)             │
│  │  │     │  ├─ charge_card (180ms)     ← BOTTLENECK        │
│  │  │     │  └─ send notifications-events (2ms)             │
│  │  │     │     └─ notification-sender: receive (50ms)      │
│  │  │     │        ├─ render_email (10ms)                   │
│  │  │     │        └─ send_email (35ms)                     │
│  │                                                           │
│  Total E2E: 397ms | Status: ✅ Within SLA (5 min)           │
│  Bottleneck: charge_card (45% of total time)                │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

💻 COMPLETE WORKING CODE

File Structure

kafka-observability-project/
├── tracing/
│   ├── trace_context.py
│   ├── kafka_tracer.py
│   ├── traced_producer.py
│   ├── traced_consumer.py
│   └── sampling.py
├── correlation/
│   ├── correlation_manager.py
│   └── correlation_store.py
├── metrics/
│   ├── e2e_latency_tracker.py
│   ├── lag_predictor.py
│   └── sla_tracker.py
├── lineage/
│   └── topic_lineage.py
├── logging_config/
│   └── structured_logger.py

│   └── sla_definitions.yaml
├── tests/
│   ├── test_tracing.py

├── demo/

└── requirements.txt

1. Trace Context Propagation

# tracing/trace_context.py
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# W3C Trace Context propagation for Kafka messages
# Handles injection into and extraction from Kafka headers
#
# Standard: https://www.w3.org/TR/trace-context/
# Header: "traceparent" = "00-{trace_id}-{span_id}-{flags}"
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

import os
import random
import struct
import time
import logging
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional

logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format="%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(message)s")
logger = logging.getLogger("trace-context")


def _generate_id(length: int) -> str:
    """Generate a random hex ID of specified byte length."""
    return os.urandom(length).hex()


def generate_trace_id() -> str:
    """Generate a 128-bit (32 hex chars) trace ID."""
    return _generate_id(16)


def generate_span_id() -> str:
    """Generate a 64-bit (16 hex chars) span ID."""
    return _generate_id(8)


@dataclass
class SpanContext:
    """
    Represents the context of a span in a distributed trace.

    Fields:
        trace_id: 128-bit identifier for the entire trace
        span_id: 64-bit identifier for this specific span
        parent_span_id: 64-bit identifier of the parent span (None for root)
        trace_flags: Sampling flags ("01" = sampled, "00" = not sampled)
        is_remote: Whether this context was extracted from a remote source
    """
    trace_id: str
    span_id: str
    parent_span_id: Optional[str] = None
    trace_flags: str = "01"
    is_remote: bool = False

    @property
    def is_sampled(self) -> bool:
        return self.trace_flags == "01"

    @property
    def traceparent(self) -> str:
        """Format as W3C traceparent header value."""
        return f"00-{self.trace_id}-{self.span_id}-{self.trace_flags}"

    @staticmethod
    def from_traceparent(header_value: str) -> Optional["SpanContext"]:
        """Parse W3C traceparent header into SpanContext."""
        try:
            parts = header_value.strip().split("-")
            if len(parts) != 4:
                return None
            if parts[0] != "00":
                return None

            trace_id = parts[1]
            span_id = parts[2]
            flags = parts[3]

            # Validate lengths
            if len(trace_id) != 32 or len(span_id) != 16:
                return None

            return SpanContext(
                trace_id=trace_id,
                span_id=span_id,
                trace_flags=flags,
                is_remote=True,
            )
        except Exception:
            return None

    def create_child(self) -> "SpanContext":
        """Create a child span context (same trace, new span)."""
        return SpanContext(
            trace_id=self.trace_id,
            span_id=generate_span_id(),
            parent_span_id=self.span_id,
            trace_flags=self.trace_flags,
            is_remote=False,
        )


@dataclass
class Span:
    """
    Represents a single operation in a distributed trace.

    A span has:
    - Context (trace_id, span_id, parent)
    - Name (what operation this represents)
    - Timestamps (start, end)
    - Attributes (key-value metadata)
    - Status (ok, error)
    - Events (timestamped annotations)
    """
    context: SpanContext
    name: str
    service_name: str
    kind: str = "INTERNAL"         # PRODUCER, CONSUMER, INTERNAL, CLIENT, SERVER
    start_time_ns: int = 0
    end_time_ns: int = 0
    attributes: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
    events: list = field(default_factory=list)
    status: str = "OK"             # OK, ERROR
    error_message: Optional[str] = None

    def __post_init__(self):
        if self.start_time_ns == 0:
            self.start_time_ns = time.time_ns()

    def set_attribute(self, key: str, value):
        """Add an attribute to the span."""
        self.attributes[key] = value

    def add_event(self, name: str, attributes: Optional[dict] = None):
        """Add a timestamped event to the span."""
        self.events.append({
            "name": name,
            "timestamp_ns": time.time_ns(),
            "attributes": attributes or {},
        })

    def set_error(self, error: Exception):
        """Mark span as errored."""
        self.status = "ERROR"
        self.error_message = str(error)
        self.set_attribute("error.type", type(error).__name__)
        self.set_attribute("error.message", str(error))

    def end(self):
        """End the span (record end timestamp)."""
        self.end_time_ns = time.time_ns()

    @property
    def duration_ms(self) -> float:
        """Span duration in milliseconds."""
        if self.end_time_ns == 0:
            return (time.time_ns() - self.start_time_ns) / 1_000_000
        return (self.end_time_ns - self.start_time_ns) / 1_000_000

    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
        """Serialize span for export."""
        return {
            "trace_id": self.context.trace_id,
            "span_id": self.context.span_id,
            "parent_span_id": self.context.parent_span_id,
            "name": self.name,
            "service_name": self.service_name,
            "kind": self.kind,
            "start_time_ns": self.start_time_ns,
            "end_time_ns": self.end_time_ns,
            "duration_ms": self.duration_ms,
            "attributes": self.attributes,
            "events": self.events,
            "status": self.status,
            "error_message": self.error_message,
            "trace_flags": self.context.trace_flags,
        }


class KafkaHeaderPropagator:
    """
    Injects and extracts trace context from Kafka message headers.

    Headers used:
    - traceparent: W3C trace context
    - correlation_id: Business correlation identifier
    - source_service: Originating service name
    - source_topic: Topic the message was produced to
    """

    TRACEPARENT_HEADER = "traceparent"
    CORRELATION_ID_HEADER = "correlation_id"
    SOURCE_SERVICE_HEADER = "source_service"
    SOURCE_TOPIC_HEADER = "source_topic"

    @staticmethod
    def inject(
        headers: Optional[list],
        span_context: SpanContext,
        correlation_id: Optional[str] = None,
        service_name: Optional[str] = None,
        topic: Optional[str] = None
    ) -> list:
        """
        Inject trace context into Kafka message headers.

        Args:
            headers: Existing headers list (or None)
            span_context: Current span context to propagate
            correlation_id: Business correlation ID
            service_name: Name of the producing service
            topic: Target topic name

        Returns:
            Updated headers list
        """
        result = list(headers or [])

        # Remove existing trace headers to avoid duplicates
        trace_header_keys = {
            KafkaHeaderPropagator.TRACEPARENT_HEADER,
            KafkaHeaderPropagator.CORRELATION_ID_HEADER,
            KafkaHeaderPropagator.SOURCE_SERVICE_HEADER,
            KafkaHeaderPropagator.SOURCE_TOPIC_HEADER,
        }
        result = [
            (k, v) for k, v in result
            if k not in trace_header_keys
        ]

        # Inject traceparent
        result.append((
            KafkaHeaderPropagator.TRACEPARENT_HEADER,
            span_context.traceparent.encode("utf-8")
        ))

        # Inject correlation ID
        if correlation_id:
            result.append((
                KafkaHeaderPropagator.CORRELATION_ID_HEADER,
                correlation_id.encode("utf-8")
            ))

        # Inject service name
        if service_name:
            result.append((
                KafkaHeaderPropagator.SOURCE_SERVICE_HEADER,
                service_name.encode("utf-8")
            ))

        # Inject topic
        if topic:
            result.append((
                KafkaHeaderPropagator.SOURCE_TOPIC_HEADER,
                topic.encode("utf-8")
            ))

        return result

    @staticmethod
    def extract(headers: Optional[list]) -> tuple[Optional[SpanContext], dict]:
        """
        Extract trace context from Kafka message headers.

        Returns:
            Tuple of (SpanContext or None, metadata dict)
        """
        span_context = None
        metadata = {}

        if not headers:
            return None, metadata

        for key, value in headers:
            if value is None:
                continue

            decoded = value.decode("utf-8") if isinstance(value, bytes) else str(value)

            if key == KafkaHeaderPropagator.TRACEPARENT_HEADER:
                span_context = SpanContext.from_traceparent(decoded)

            elif key == KafkaHeaderPropagator.CORRELATION_ID_HEADER:
                metadata["correlation_id"] = decoded

            elif key == KafkaHeaderPropagator.SOURCE_SERVICE_HEADER:
                metadata["source_service"] = decoded

            elif key == KafkaHeaderPropagator.SOURCE_TOPIC_HEADER:
                metadata["source_topic"] = decoded

        return span_context, metadata

2. Sampling Strategy

# tracing/sampling.py
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Head-based sampling strategy for Kafka message tracing
# Decides at production time whether a message should be traced
#
# Strategies:
#   - Always: 100% sampling (for critical topics)
#   - Probabilistic: X% sampling (for high-volume topics)
#   - Rule-based: conditional sampling (error, high-value, etc.)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

import hashlib
import logging
import random
from typing import Optional, Callable

logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format="%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(message)s")
logger = logging.getLogger("sampling")


class SamplingDecision:
    """Result of a sampling decision."""
    SAMPLE = "01"       # Trace this message
    DROP = "00"         # Don't trace this message

    def __init__(self, sampled: bool, reason: str = ""):
        self.sampled = sampled
        self.trace_flags = self.SAMPLE if sampled else self.DROP
        self.reason = reason


class Sampler:
    """Base sampler interface."""

    def should_sample(
        self,
        topic: str,
        key: Optional[bytes] = None,
        headers: Optional[list] = None,
        attributes: Optional[dict] = None
    ) -> SamplingDecision:
        raise NotImplementedError


class AlwaysSampler(Sampler):
    """Always sample — 100% of messages traced."""

    def should_sample(self, topic, key=None, headers=None, attributes=None):
        return SamplingDecision(True, "always_on")


class NeverSampler(Sampler):
    """Never sample — 0% of messages traced."""

    def should_sample(self, topic, key=None, headers=None, attributes=None):
        return SamplingDecision(False, "always_off")


class ProbabilisticSampler(Sampler):
    """
    Sample a percentage of messages.
    Uses consistent hashing on key for deterministic sampling
    (same key always sampled or not).
    """

    def __init__(self, rate: float = 0.01):
        """
        Args:
            rate: Sampling rate (0.0 to 1.0). Default 1% = 0.01
        """
        self.rate = max(0.0, min(1.0, rate))

    def should_sample(self, topic, key=None, headers=None, attributes=None):
        if key:
            # Consistent sampling: same key → same decision
            hash_val = int(hashlib.md5(key).hexdigest(), 16) % 10000
            sampled = hash_val < (self.rate * 10000)
        else:
            # Random sampling for keyless messages
            sampled = random.random() < self.rate

        return SamplingDecision(sampled, f"probabilistic({self.rate})")


class RuleBasedSampler(Sampler):
    """
    Sample based on configurable rules per topic.

    Rules evaluated in order — first match wins.
    """

    def __init__(self, rules: Optional[dict] = None, default_rate: float = 0.01):
        """
        Args:
            rules: Dict mapping topic pattern → sampling rate
                   Example: {"payments.*": 1.0, "analytics.*": 0.001}
            default_rate: Rate for topics not matching any rule
        """
        self.rules = rules or {}
        self.default_rate = default_rate

        # Pre-compile rules
        self._compiled_rules = []
        for pattern, rate in self.rules.items():
            import re
            regex = re.compile(pattern.replace("*", ".*"))
            self._compiled_rules.append((regex, rate))

        logger.info(
            f"RuleBasedSampler initialized with {len(self._compiled_rules)} rules, "
            f"default_rate={default_rate}"
        )

    def should_sample(self, topic, key=None, headers=None, attributes=None):
        # Check for force-sample conditions
        if attributes and attributes.get("error"):
            return SamplingDecision(True, "error_always_sampled")

        if attributes and attributes.get("force_sample"):
            return SamplingDecision(True, "force_sampled")

        # Find matching rule
        rate = self.default_rate
        matched_rule = "default"

        for regex, rule_rate in self._compiled_rules:
            if regex.match(topic):
                rate = rule_rate
                matched_rule = regex.pattern
                break

        # Apply probabilistic sampling at matched rate
        if rate >= 1.0:
            return SamplingDecision(True, f"rule({matched_rule})=100%")
        elif rate <= 0.0:
            return SamplingDecision(False, f"rule({matched_rule})=0%")

        if key:
            hash_val = int(hashlib.md5(key).hexdigest(), 16) % 10000
            sampled = hash_val < (rate * 10000)
        else:
            sampled = random.random() < rate

        return SamplingDecision(sampled, f"rule({matched_rule})={rate}")


def create_sampler(config: dict) -> Sampler:
    """Factory: create sampler from configuration."""
    strategy = config.get("strategy", "rule_based")

    if strategy == "always":
        return AlwaysSampler()
    elif strategy == "never":
        return NeverSampler()
    elif strategy == "probabilistic":
        return ProbabilisticSampler(rate=config.get("rate", 0.01))
    elif strategy == "rule_based":
        return RuleBasedSampler(
            rules=config.get("rules", {}),
            default_rate=config.get("default_rate", 0.01),
        )
    else:
        return ProbabilisticSampler(rate=0.01)

3. Kafka Tracer (Span Collector and Exporter)

# tracing/kafka_tracer.py
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Central tracer that manages spans and exports them
# Provides the main API for creating and managing traces
# Exports to OpenTelemetry Collector or console
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

import json
import logging
import threading
import time
import queue
from typing import Optional, Callable
from dataclasses import dataclass

from trace_context import SpanContext, Span, generate_trace_id, generate_span_id
from sampling import Sampler, RuleBasedSampler, SamplingDecision

logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format="%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(message)s")
logger = logging.getLogger("kafka-tracer")


class SpanExporter:
    """Base interface for span exporters."""

    def export(self, spans: list[Span]):
        raise NotImplementedError

    def shutdown(self):
        pass


class ConsoleSpanExporter(SpanExporter):
    """Exports spans to console (for development/debugging)."""

    def export(self, spans: list[Span]):
        for span in spans:
            duration = span.duration_ms
            parent = span.context.parent_span_id or "ROOT"
            status_emoji = "✅" if span.status == "OK" else "❌"

            logger.info(
                f"{status_emoji} SPAN | "
                f"trace={span.context.trace_id[:8]}... | "
                f"span={span.context.span_id[:8]} | "
                f"parent={parent[:8]} | "
                f"service={span.service_name} | "
                f"name={span.name} | "
                f"kind={span.kind} | "
                f"duration={duration:.1f}ms | "
                f"attrs={json.dumps(span.attributes)}"
            )


class OTLPSpanExporter(SpanExporter):
    """
    Exports spans to OpenTelemetry Collector via OTLP/HTTP.

    In production, use the official opentelemetry-sdk package.
    This is a simplified implementation showing the wire format.
    """

    def __init__(self, endpoint: str = "http://localhost:4318/v1/traces"):
        self.endpoint = endpoint

    def export(self, spans: list[Span]):
        """Export spans to OTLP endpoint."""
        try:
            import requests

            # Build OTLP trace export request
            resource_spans = self._build_resource_spans(spans)
            payload = {"resourceSpans": resource_spans}

            response = requests.post(
                self.endpoint,
                json=payload,
                headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"},
                timeout=5,
            )

            if response.status_code != 200:
                logger.warning(
                    f"OTLP export failed: {response.status_code} {response.text}"
                )

        except ImportError:
            logger.debug("requests not installed — OTLP export skipped")
        except Exception as e:
            logger.debug(f"OTLP export error: {e}")

    def _build_resource_spans(self, spans: list[Span]) -> list:
        """Build OTLP ResourceSpans structure."""
        # Group spans by service
        by_service = {}
        for span in spans:
            svc = span.service_name
            if svc not in by_service:
                by_service[svc] = []
            by_service[svc].append(span)

        resource_spans = []
        for service_name, service_spans in by_service.items():
            scope_spans = []
            for span in service_spans:
                otlp_span = {
                    "traceId": span.context.trace_id,
                    "spanId": span.context.span_id,
                    "parentSpanId": span.context.parent_span_id or "",
                    "name": span.name,
                    "kind": self._map_kind(span.kind),
                    "startTimeUnixNano": str(span.start_time_ns),
                    "endTimeUnixNano": str(span.end_time_ns),
                    "attributes": [
                        {"key": k, "value": {"stringValue": str(v)}}
                        for k, v in span.attributes.items()
                    ],
                    "status": {
                        "code": 1 if span.status == "OK" else 2,
                        "message": span.error_message or "",
                    },
                }
                scope_spans.append(otlp_span)

            resource_spans.append({
                "resource": {
                    "attributes": [
                        {"key": "service.name", "value": {"stringValue": service_name}},
                    ]
                },
                "scopeSpans": [{
                    "scope": {"name": "kafka-tracer", "version": "1.0.0"},
                    "spans": scope_spans,
                }],
            })

        return resource_spans

    @staticmethod
    def _map_kind(kind: str) -> int:
        return {
            "INTERNAL": 1,
            "SERVER": 2,
            "CLIENT": 3,
            "PRODUCER": 4,
            "CONSUMER": 5,
        }.get(kind, 1)


class KafkaTracer:
    """
    Central tracer for Kafka message tracing.

    Responsibilities:
    1. Create spans for produce/consume operations
    2. Manage trace context propagation
    3. Apply sampling decisions
    4. Batch export spans to backend
    5. Track active spans for context management

    Thread-safe: multiple producers/consumers can use concurrently.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        service_name: str,
        sampler: Optional[Sampler] = None,
        exporter: Optional[SpanExporter] = None,
        batch_size: int = 100,
        export_interval_seconds: float = 5.0
    ):
        self.service_name = service_name
        self.sampler = sampler or RuleBasedSampler()
        self.exporter = exporter or ConsoleSpanExporter()

        # Span buffer for batch export
        self._span_queue = queue.Queue(maxsize=10000)
        self._batch_size = batch_size
        self._export_interval = export_interval_seconds

        # Active spans (thread-local for context management)
        self._active_spans = threading.local()

        # Background export thread
        self._running = True
        self._export_thread = threading.Thread(
            target=self._export_loop,
            daemon=True,
            name="span-exporter"
        )
        self._export_thread.start()

        # Metrics
        self._lock = threading.Lock()
        self._metrics = {
            "spans_created": 0,
            "spans_exported": 0,
            "spans_dropped": 0,
            "spans_sampled": 0,
            "spans_not_sampled": 0,
        }

        logger.info(
            f"KafkaTracer initialized: service={service_name}, "
            f"sampler={type(self.sampler).__name__}"
        )

    def start_producer_span(
        self,
        topic: str,
        key: Optional[bytes] = None,
        parent_context: Optional[SpanContext] = None,
        correlation_id: Optional[str] = None,
        attributes: Optional[dict] = None
    ) -> Span:
        """
        Create a producer span for a message being sent.

        Args:
            topic: Target topic
            key: Message key
            parent_context: Parent span context (for chained produces)
            correlation_id: Business correlation ID
            attributes: Additional span attributes

        Returns:
            Span object (call .end() when produce is complete)
        """
        # Sampling decision
        decision = self.sampler.should_sample(
            topic=topic,
            key=key,
            attributes=attributes,
        )

        with self._lock:
            if decision.sampled:
                self._metrics["spans_sampled"] += 1
            else:
                self._metrics["spans_not_sampled"] += 1

        # Create span context
        if parent_context:
            context = parent_context.create_child()
        else:
            context = SpanContext(
                trace_id=generate_trace_id(),
                span_id=generate_span_id(),
                trace_flags=decision.trace_flags,
            )

        # Create span
        span = Span(
            context=context,
            name=f"{topic} send",
            service_name=self.service_name,
            kind="PRODUCER",
        )

        # Set standard attributes
        span.set_attribute("messaging.system", "kafka")
        span.set_attribute("messaging.destination", topic)
        span.set_attribute("messaging.operation", "send")
        span.set_attribute("sampling.decision", decision.reason)

        if key:
            span.set_attribute("messaging.kafka.message.key",
                             key.decode("utf-8", errors="replace"))
        if correlation_id:
            span.set_attribute("correlation_id", correlation_id)
        if attributes:
            for k, v in attributes.items():
                span.set_attribute(k, v)

        with self._lock:
            self._metrics["spans_created"] += 1

        return span

    def start_consumer_span(
        self,
        msg,
        parent_context: Optional[SpanContext] = None,
        correlation_id: Optional[str] = None
    ) -> Span:
        """
        Create a consumer span for a message being received.

        Args:
            msg: Kafka message (from consumer.poll())
            parent_context: Extracted parent context from message headers
            correlation_id: Extracted correlation ID

        Returns:
            Span object (call .end() when processing is complete)
        """
        # Create child context from parent (if available)
        if parent_context:
            context = parent_context.create_child()
        else:
            context = SpanContext(
                trace_id=generate_trace_id(),
                span_id=generate_span_id(),
                trace_flags="01",
            )

        span = Span(
            context=context,
            name=f"{msg.topic()} receive",
            service_name=self.service_name,
            kind="CONSUMER",
        )

        # Set standard attributes
        span.set_attribute("messaging.system", "kafka")
        span.set_attribute("messaging.source", msg.topic())
        span.set_attribute("messaging.operation", "receive")
        span.set_attribute("messaging.kafka.partition", msg.partition())
        span.set_attribute("messaging.kafka.offset", msg.offset())
        span.set_attribute("messaging.kafka.consumer.group", "unknown")

        if msg.key():
            span.set_attribute("messaging.kafka.message.key",
                             msg.key().decode("utf-8", errors="replace"))
        if correlation_id:
            span.set_attribute("correlation_id", correlation_id)

        # Calculate broker-to-consumer latency
        msg_timestamp = msg.timestamp()
        if msg_timestamp and msg_timestamp[0] > 0:
            produce_time_ms = msg_timestamp[1]
            receive_time_ms = time.time() * 1000
            broker_latency_ms = receive_time_ms - produce_time_ms
            span.set_attribute("messaging.kafka.broker_latency_ms",
                             round(broker_latency_ms, 1))

        with self._lock:
            self._metrics["spans_created"] += 1

        # Store as active span for this thread
        self._set_active_span(span)

        return span

    def start_processing_span(
        self,
        name: str,
        parent_span: Optional[Span] = None,
        attributes: Optional[dict] = None
    ) -> Span:
        """
        Create an internal processing span (child of consumer span).

        Used for tracking individual processing steps:
        - database insert
        - API call
        - validation logic
        """
        parent = parent_span or self._get_active_span()

        if parent:
            context = parent.context.create_child()
        else:
            context = SpanContext(
                trace_id=generate_trace_id(),
                span_id=generate_span_id(),
            )

        span = Span(
            context=context,
            name=name,
            service_name=self.service_name,
            kind="INTERNAL",
        )

        if attributes:
            for k, v in attributes.items():
                span.set_attribute(k, v)

        with self._lock:
            self._metrics["spans_created"] += 1

        return span

    def finish_span(self, span: Span):
        """
        End a span and queue it for export.
        Must be called for every span created.
        """
        span.end()

        # Only export sampled spans
        if span.context.is_sampled:
            try:
                self._span_queue.put_nowait(span)
            except queue.Full:
                with self._lock:
                    self._metrics["spans_dropped"] += 1

    def _export_loop(self):
        """Background thread: batch export spans."""
        while self._running:
            try:
                batch = []
                deadline = time.time() + self._export_interval

                while time.time() < deadline and len(batch) < self._batch_size:
                    try:
                        span = self._span_queue.get(timeout=0.5)
                        batch.append(span)
                    except queue.Empty:
                        break

                if batch:
                    self.exporter.export(batch)
                    with self._lock:
                        self._metrics["spans_exported"] += len(batch)

            except Exception as e:
                logger.warning(f"Span export error: {e}")

    def _set_active_span(self, span: Span):
        """Set the active span for the current thread."""
        if not hasattr(self._active_spans, 'stack'):
            self._active_spans.stack = []
        self._active_spans.stack.append(span)

    def _get_active_span(self) -> Optional[Span]:
        """Get the active span for the current thread."""
        if hasattr(self._active_spans, 'stack') and self._active_spans.stack:
            return self._active_spans.stack[-1]
        return None

    def _pop_active_span(self) -> Optional[Span]:
        """Remove and return the active span for the current thread."""
        if hasattr(self._active_spans, 'stack') and self._active_spans.stack:
            return self._active_spans.stack.pop()
        return None

    def get_metrics(self) -> dict:
        """Get tracer metrics."""
        with self._lock:
            return dict(self._metrics)

    def shutdown(self):
        """Graceful shutdown — flush remaining spans."""
        self._running = False

        # Export remaining spans
        remaining = []
        while not self._span_queue.empty():
            try:
                remaining.append(self._span_queue.get_nowait())
            except queue.Empty:
                break

        if remaining:
            self.exporter.export(remaining)
            with self._lock:
                self._metrics["spans_exported"] += len(remaining)

        self.exporter.shutdown()
        logger.info(f"Tracer shutdown: {self._metrics}")

4. Traced Producer

# tracing/traced_producer.py
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Kafka producer with automatic distributed tracing
# Wraps confluent_kafka.Producer with trace context injection
#
# Every message sent automatically:
# 1. Gets a producer span created
# 2. Gets trace context injected into headers
# 3. Gets correlation ID propagated
# 4. Reports delivery status to span
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

import json
import logging
import time
from typing import Optional, Callable

from confluent_kafka import Producer

from trace_context import SpanContext, Span, KafkaHeaderPropagator
from kafka_tracer import KafkaTracer

logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format="%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(message)s")
logger = logging.getLogger("traced-producer")


class TracedProducer:
    """
    Kafka producer with built-in distributed tracing.

    Usage:
        tracer = KafkaTracer(service_name="order-api")
        producer = TracedProducer(
            bootstrap_servers="localhost:9092",
            tracer=tracer,
        )

        producer.send(
            topic="orders-events",
            key=b"ORDER-123",
            value=json.dumps(order).encode(),
            correlation_id="ORDER-123",
        )
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        bootstrap_servers: str,
        tracer: KafkaTracer,
        producer_config: Optional[dict] = None
    ):
        self.tracer = tracer

        config = {
            "bootstrap.servers": bootstrap_servers,
            "acks": "all",
            "enable.idempotence": True,
            "linger.ms": 10,
            "compression.type": "lz4",
        }
        if producer_config:
            config.update(producer_config)

        self._producer = Producer(config)

    def send(
        self,
        topic: str,
        value: bytes,
        key: Optional[bytes] = None,
        headers: Optional[list] = None,
        correlation_id: Optional[str] = None,
        parent_context: Optional[SpanContext] = None,
        attributes: Optional[dict] = None,
        on_delivery: Optional[Callable] = None
    ) -> Span:
        """
        Send a message with automatic trace context injection.

        Args:
            topic: Target topic
            value: Message value
            key: Message key
            headers: Additional headers
            correlation_id: Business correlation ID
            parent_context: Parent span context for chained operations
            attributes: Additional span attributes
            on_delivery: User delivery callback

        Returns:
            The producer span (already started, ended on delivery)
        """
        # Create producer span
        extra_attrs = dict(attributes or {})
        extra_attrs["messaging.kafka.message.size"] = len(value)

        span = self.tracer.start_producer_span(
            topic=topic,
            key=key,
            parent_context=parent_context,
            correlation_id=correlation_id,
            attributes=extra_attrs,
        )

        # Inject trace context into headers
        traced_headers = KafkaHeaderPropagator.inject(
            headers=headers,
            span_context=span.context,
            correlation_id=correlation_id,
            service_name=self.tracer.service_name,
            topic=topic,
        )

        # Create delivery callback that ends the span
        def _traced_delivery_callback(err, msg):
            if err:
                span.set_error(Exception(str(err)))
                span.add_event("delivery_failed", {"error": str(err)})
            else:
                span.set_attribute("messaging.kafka.partition", msg.partition())
                span.set_attribute("messaging.kafka.offset", msg.offset())
                span.add_event("delivered", {
                    "partition": msg.partition(),
                    "offset": msg.offset(),
                })

            self.tracer.finish_span(span)

            # Call user callback if provided
            if on_delivery:
                on_delivery(err, msg)

        # Send message
        try:
            kwargs = {
                "topic": topic,
                "value": value,
                "headers": traced_headers,
                "on_delivery": _traced_delivery_callback,
            }
            if key is not None:
                kwargs["key"] = key

            self._producer.produce(**kwargs)
            self._producer.poll(0)

        except Exception as e:
            span.set_error(e)
            self.tracer.finish_span(span)
            raise

        return span

    def flush(self, timeout: float = 10.0) -> int:
        return self._producer.flush(timeout=timeout)

    def close(self):
        self._producer.flush(timeout=30)

5. Traced Consumer

# tracing/traced_consumer.py
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Kafka consumer with automatic distributed tracing
# Extracts trace context from message headers
# Creates consumer spans and processing spans automatically
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

import logging
import time
from typing import Optional, Callable

from confluent_kafka import Consumer, KafkaError, TopicPartition

from trace_context import SpanContext, Span, KafkaHeaderPropagator
from kafka_tracer import KafkaTracer

logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format="%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(message)s")
logger = logging.getLogger("traced-consumer")


class TracedMessage:
    """
    Wraps a Kafka message with trace context extracted from headers.

    Provides easy access to:
    - Original message fields
    - Parent span context (for creating child spans)
    - Correlation ID
    - Source service information
    """

    def __init__(self, msg, parent_context, metadata, consumer_span):
        self._msg = msg
        self.parent_context = parent_context
        self.correlation_id = metadata.get("correlation_id")
        self.source_service = metadata.get("source_service")
        self.source_topic = metadata.get("source_topic")
        self.consumer_span = consumer_span

    # Delegate to original message
    def topic(self):
        return self._msg.topic()

    def partition(self):
        return self._msg.partition()

    def offset(self):
        return self._msg.offset()

    def key(self):
        return self._msg.key()

    def value(self):
        return self._msg.value()

    def headers(self):
        return self._msg.headers()

    def timestamp(self):
        return self._msg.timestamp()

    def error(self):
        return self._msg.error()


class TracedConsumer:
    """
    Kafka consumer with automatic trace context extraction.

    Usage:
        tracer = KafkaTracer(service_name="order-processor")
        consumer = TracedConsumer(
            bootstrap_servers="localhost:9092",
            group_id="order-processor",
            topics=["orders-events"],
            tracer=tracer,
        )

        for traced_msg in consumer.consume():
            # traced_msg has .correlation_id, .parent_context, .consumer_span
            process(traced_msg)
            traced_msg.consumer_span.end()
            tracer.finish_span(traced_msg.consumer_span)
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        bootstrap_servers: str,
        group_id: str,
        topics: list[str],
        tracer: KafkaTracer,
        consumer_config: Optional[dict] = None
    ):
        self.tracer = tracer
        self.group_id = group_id

        config = {
            "bootstrap.servers": bootstrap_servers,
            "group.id": group_id,
            "auto.offset.reset": "latest",
            "enable.auto.commit": False,
            "max.poll.interval.ms": 300000,
            "session.timeout.ms": 30000,
        }
        if consumer_config:
            config.update(consumer_config)

        self._consumer = Consumer(config)
        self._consumer.subscribe(topics)
        self._running = True

    def poll(self, timeout: float = 1.0) -> Optional[TracedMessage]:
        """
        Poll for a single message with trace context extraction.

        Returns:
            TracedMessage with trace context, or None
        """
        msg = self._consumer.poll(timeout=timeout)

        if msg is None:
            return None

        if msg.error():
            if msg.error().code() == KafkaError._PARTITION_EOF:
                return None
            logger.warning(f"Consumer error: {msg.error()}")
            return None

        # Extract trace context from headers
        parent_context, metadata = KafkaHeaderPropagator.extract(msg.headers())

        # Create consumer span
        consumer_span = self.tracer.start_consumer_span(
            msg=msg,
            parent_context=parent_context,
            correlation_id=metadata.get("correlation_id"),
        )

        # Add consumer group to span
        consumer_span.set_attribute("messaging.kafka.consumer.group", self.group_id)

        if metadata.get("source_service"):
            consumer_span.set_attribute("messaging.source_service",
                                       metadata["source_service"])

        return TracedMessage(msg, parent_context, metadata, consumer_span)

    def consume(self, poll_timeout: float = 1.0):
        """
        Generator: yields TracedMessage objects.

        Usage:
            for traced_msg in consumer.consume():
                process(traced_msg)
                consumer.complete(traced_msg)
        """
        while self._running:
            traced_msg = self.poll(timeout=poll_timeout)
            if traced_msg:
                yield traced_msg

    def complete(self, traced_msg: TracedMessage, error: Optional[Exception] = None):
        """
        Complete processing of a traced message.
        Ends the consumer span and commits the offset.
        """
        if error:
            traced_msg.consumer_span.set_error(error)

        self.tracer.finish_span(traced_msg.consumer_span)

        try:
            self._consumer.commit(asynchronous=True)
        except Exception as e:
            logger.warning(f"Commit failed: {e}")

    def close(self):
        """Shutdown consumer."""
        self._running = False
        try:
            self._consumer.commit(asynchronous=False)
        except Exception:
            pass
        self._consumer.close()

6. End-to-End Latency Tracker

# metrics/e2e_latency_tracker.py
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Tracks end-to-end latency for messages flowing through pipelines
# Calculates P50, P95, P99 latencies per pipeline
# Exports as Prometheus metrics
#
# How it works:
# 1. Producer records produce timestamp in message header
# 2. Final consumer reads timestamp and calculates delta
# 3. Delta = end-to-end latency for that pipeline
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

import time
import logging
import threading
from collections import defaultdict
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional

logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format="%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(message)s")
logger = logging.getLogger("e2e-latency")


@dataclass
class LatencyBucket:
    """Stores latency samples for a pipeline with percentile calculation."""
    pipeline_name: str
    samples: list = field(default_factory=list)
    max_samples: int = 10000
    _lock: threading.Lock = field(default_factory=threading.Lock)

    def record(self, latency_ms: float):
        with self._lock:
            self.samples.append(latency_ms)
            if len(self.samples) > self.max_samples:
                # Keep recent samples only
                self.samples = self.samples[-self.max_samples:]

    def percentile(self, pct: float) -> float:
        """Calculate percentile (0-100) from samples."""
        with self._lock:
            if not self.samples:
                return 0.0
            sorted_samples = sorted(self.samples)
            index = int(len(sorted_samples) * pct / 100)
            index = min(index, len(sorted_samples) - 1)
            return sorted_samples[index]

    @property
    def p50(self) -> float:
        return self.percentile(50)

    @property
    def p95(self) -> float:
        return self.percentile(95)

    @property
    def p99(self) -> float:
        return self.percentile(99)

    @property
    def count(self) -> int:
        with self._lock:
            return len(self.samples)

    @property
    def mean(self) -> float:
        with self._lock:
            if not self.samples:
                return 0.0
            return sum(self.samples) / len(self.samples)

    def get_stats(self) -> dict:
        return {
            "pipeline": self.pipeline_name,
            "count": self.count,
            "mean_ms": round(self.mean, 2),
            "p50_ms": round(self.p50, 2),
            "p95_ms": round(self.p95, 2),
            "p99_ms": round(self.p99, 2),
        }


class EndToEndLatencyTracker:
    """
    Tracks end-to-end latency across Kafka pipelines.

    A "pipeline" is a named path from producer to final consumer:
    Example: "order-to-notification"
      order-api → orders-events → order-processor →
      payments-events → payment-processor →
      notifications-events → notification-sender

    Latency = notification-sender receive time - order-api produce time

    Implementation:
    - Producer injects "e2e.origin_timestamp_ms" header
    - Each intermediate service propagates the header
    - Final consumer reads the header and calculates delta
    """

    ORIGIN_TIMESTAMP_HEADER = "e2e.origin_timestamp_ms"
    PIPELINE_NAME_HEADER = "e2e.pipeline_name"

    def __init__(self):
        self._buckets: dict[str, LatencyBucket] = {}
        self._lock = threading.Lock()
        self._sla_breaches = defaultdict(int)

    def inject_origin_timestamp(
        self,
        headers: Optional[list],
        pipeline_name: str
    ) -> list:
        """
        Inject origin timestamp into message headers.
        Called by the FIRST producer in a pipeline.

        Only injects if not already present (preserves original timestamp
        when message flows through intermediate services).
        """
        result = list(headers or [])

        # Check if origin timestamp already exists
        existing_keys = {h[0] for h in result}
        if self.ORIGIN_TIMESTAMP_HEADER not in existing_keys:
            result.append((
                self.ORIGIN_TIMESTAMP_HEADER,
                str(int(time.time() * 1000)).encode("utf-8")
            ))

        if self.PIPELINE_NAME_HEADER not in existing_keys:
            result.append((
                self.PIPELINE_NAME_HEADER,
                pipeline_name.encode("utf-8")
            ))

        return result

    def record_arrival(
        self,
        headers: Optional[list],
        pipeline_name: Optional[str] = None,
        sla_threshold_ms: Optional[float] = None
    ) -> Optional[float]:
        """
        Record message arrival at the final consumer.
        Calculates E2E latency from origin timestamp.

        Args:
            headers: Message headers containing origin timestamp
            pipeline_name: Override pipeline name (if not in headers)
            sla_threshold_ms: SLA threshold — record breach if exceeded

        Returns:
            Latency in milliseconds, or None if no origin timestamp
        """
        if not headers:
            return None

        origin_ms = None
        header_pipeline = None

        for key, value in headers:
            if value is None:
                continue
            decoded = value.decode("utf-8") if isinstance(value, bytes) else str(value)

            if key == self.ORIGIN_TIMESTAMP_HEADER:
                try:
                    origin_ms = float(decoded)
                except ValueError:
                    pass

            elif key == self.PIPELINE_NAME_HEADER:
                header_pipeline = decoded

        if origin_ms is None:
            return None

        # Calculate latency
        now_ms = time.time() * 1000
        latency_ms = now_ms - origin_ms

        # Determine pipeline name
        name = pipeline_name or header_pipeline or "unknown"

        # Record in bucket
        with self._lock:
            if name not in self._buckets:
                self._buckets[name] = LatencyBucket(pipeline_name=name)
            self._buckets[name].record(latency_ms)

        # Check SLA
        if sla_threshold_ms is not None and latency_ms > sla_threshold_ms:
            with self._lock:
                self._sla_breaches[name] += 1
            logger.warning(
                f"SLA BREACH: pipeline={name}, "
                f"latency={latency_ms:.0f}ms > threshold={sla_threshold_ms:.0f}ms"
            )

        return latency_ms

    def get_pipeline_stats(self, pipeline_name: str) -> Optional[dict]:
        """Get latency stats for a specific pipeline."""
        with self._lock:
            bucket = self._buckets.get(pipeline_name)
            if bucket:
                stats = bucket.get_stats()
                stats["sla_breaches"] = self._sla_breaches.get(pipeline_name, 0)
                return stats
            return None

    def get_all_stats(self) -> dict:
        """Get latency stats for all pipelines."""
        with self._lock:
            return {
                name: {
                    **bucket.get_stats(),
                    "sla_breaches": self._sla_breaches.get(name, 0),
                }
                for name, bucket in self._buckets.items()
            }

    def get_prometheus_metrics(self) -> str:
        """
        Export metrics in Prometheus exposition format.
        Can be served via HTTP endpoint for Prometheus scraping.
        """
        lines = []
        lines.append("# HELP kafka_pipeline_e2e_latency_ms End-to-end pipeline latency")
        lines.append("# TYPE kafka_pipeline_e2e_latency_ms summary")

        with self._lock:
            for name, bucket in self._buckets.items():
                safe_name = name.replace("-", "_")
                lines.append(
                    f'kafka_pipeline_e2e_latency_ms{{pipeline="{name}",quantile="0.5"}} '
                    f'{bucket.p50:.2f}'
                )
                lines.append(
                    f'kafka_pipeline_e2e_latency_ms{{pipeline="{name}",quantile="0.95"}} '
                    f'{bucket.p95:.2f}'
                )
                lines.append(
                    f'kafka_pipeline_e2e_latency_ms{{pipeline="{name}",quantile="0.99"}} '
                    f'{bucket.p99:.2f}'
                )
                lines.append(
                    f'kafka_pipeline_e2e_latency_ms_count{{pipeline="{name}"}} '
                    f'{bucket.count}'
                )

            lines.append("")
            lines.append("# HELP kafka_pipeline_sla_breaches_total SLA breach count")
            lines.append("# TYPE kafka_pipeline_sla_breaches_total counter")
            for name, count in self._sla_breaches.items():
                lines.append(
                    f'kafka_pipeline_sla_breaches_total{{pipeline="{name}"}} {count}'
                )

        return "\n".join(lines)

7. Consumer Lag Predictor

# metrics/lag_predictor.py
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Predicts future consumer lag using linear regression
# Alerts BEFORE SLA breach instead of after
#
# Algorithm:
# 1. Collect lag samples over time
# 2. Fit linear regression to recent samples
# 3. If slope > 0: calculate time-to-breach
# 4. Alert if time-to-breach < threshold
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

import time
import logging
import threading
import math
from collections import defaultdict
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional
