   JMX metrics + Prometheus + Grafana provided METRIC observability.
   This project adds the other two pillars: TRACES and LOGS.
   All three pillars connected via trace_id and correlation_id.

← Project 41 (Custom Interceptors):
   Interceptors are the ideal hook point for automatic tracing.
   onSend() → create producer span, inject headers
   onConsume() → extract headers, create consumer span
   Application code doesn't change when tracing is added.

← Project 44 (Fraud Detection / CEP):
   Complex multi-stream processing needs trace visibility.
   When a fraud alert fires, trace shows exactly which events
   contributed and how they flowed through the system.

← Project 48 (Backpressure):
   Backpressure events (pause/resume, spillover, circuit breaker)
   need visibility. This project's SLA tracker detects when
   backpressure causes SLA breaches. Lag predictor warns
   BEFORE the backpressure cascade begins.

← Project 47 (DR Failover):
   Cross-cluster trace propagation via MM2-replicated headers.
   E2E latency measurement spans the failover boundary.
   SLA dashboard shows impact of failover on compliance.

WHAT THIS PROJECT INTRODUCED:

1. W3C Trace Context Propagation:
   → traceparent header: "00-{trace_id}-{span_id}-{flags}"
   → Injected by producer, extracted by consumer
   → Same trace_id flows through entire pipeline
   → Standard format works with any tracing backend

2. Head-Based Sampling Strategy:
   → Rule-based: 100% for payments, 1% for analytics
   → Consistent hashing: same key always sampled or not
   → Error force-sampling: errors always traced
   → Reduces storage cost while preserving critical traces

3. Traced Producer & Consumer:
   → Automatic span creation on produce/consume
   → Trace context injected/extracted transparently
   → Processing sub-spans for detailed timing breakdown
   → Delivery callback ends producer span with result

4. End-to-End Latency Tracking:
   → Origin timestamp injected at first producer
   → Propagated through all intermediate services
   → Final consumer calculates total pipeline latency
   → P50/P95/P99 percentiles per pipeline

5. Consumer Lag Prediction:
   → Linear regression on lag time series
   → Calculates slope (records/second change rate)
   → Predicts time-to-breach based on current trend
   → Alerts BEFORE SLA breach, not after
   → R² confidence score filters noisy predictions

6. SLA Tracking as Code:
   → YAML definitions: "99.9% within 5 minutes"
   → Real-time compliance calculation
   → Business-level commitments mapped to technical metrics
   → Executive dashboard with GREEN/YELLOW/RED status

7. Topic Lineage Registry:
   → Dynamic graph built from producer/consumer activity
   → Upstream query: "What feeds this topic?"
   → Downstream query: "What depends on this topic?"
   → Impact analysis: "If this fails, what breaks?"
   → Blast radius calculation for incident response

8. Structured JSON Logging:
   → Every log line includes trace_id, span_id, correlation_id
   → Enables trace → log and log → trace navigation
   → Thread-local context propagation
   → Standard format across all services

9. Correlation ID Pattern:
   → Business-level ID (order_id) separate from trace_id
   → Trace_id changes per retry; correlation_id stays constant
   → "Find all events for ORDER-5678" query
   → Propagated via Kafka headers alongside trace context

BRIDGE TO PROJECT 50:
────────────────────
Project 50: "The Enterprise Event Mesh — Unified Streaming Architecture
            & Bridge to What's Next"

This final project brings EVERYTHING together:
→ All 49 projects' concepts unified into a single architecture
→ Domain-driven topic design (bounded contexts as topics)
→ Event mesh topology connecting multiple clusters
→ Governance layer (who can produce/consume what)
→ Self-serve platform (teams onboard without platform team)
→ Cost modeling (storage, compute, network per team)
→ Bridge to: Apache Flink, Spark Streaming, Apache Pulsar,
  Cloud-native eventing (AWS EventBridge, Azure Event Grid)

The connection: Observability (this project) is what makes
an enterprise event mesh OPERABLE. Without trace visibility,
lag prediction, SLA tracking, and lineage — the mesh is a
black box that nobody trusts for critical workloads.
Project 50 is the architectural capstone that turns 49
individual skills into one coherent platform strategy.

✅ PROJECT 49 COMPLETE

This project delivered:

ComponentPurpose
SpanContextW3C Trace Context with trace_id, span_id, traceparent format
SpanFull span lifecycle: attributes, events, error status, duration
KafkaHeaderPropagatorInject/extract trace context + correlation ID via Kafka headers
Sampling StrategiesAlways, Never, Probabilistic, Rule-based with consistent hashing
KafkaTracerCentral tracer: span creation, batch export, thread-local context
ConsoleSpanExporterDevelopment exporter for debugging traces
OTLPSpanExporterProduction exporter for OpenTelemetry Collector
TracedProducerKafka producer with automatic span creation and header injection
TracedConsumerKafka consumer with automatic context extraction and span creation
EndToEndLatencyTrackerPipeline latency measurement with P50/P95/P99 and SLA breach detection
LagPredictorLinear regression lag prediction with time-to-breach and confidence scoring
SLATrackerSLA-as-code with compliance tracking and executive dashboard
TopicLineageRegistryDynamic lineage graph with impact analysis and blast radius
StructuredJsonFormatterJSON logging with trace context for log-trace correlation
TraceContextFilterThread-local trace context injection into log records
Traced Pipeline Demo4-service pipeline demonstrating full observability stack
Test SuiteTests for context propagation, sampling, latency, prediction, SLA, lineage
